hints.Pastores: 'Pastores' is a noun meaning 'shepherds' — it is the subject
hints.regione: 'regione' is a noun meaning 'region' in the ablative

hints.to eat: 'to eat' is 'edere/esse' — 'erant' comes from 'esse' (to be), the irregular verb

hints.Present: Present 3rd plural of 'esse' is 'sunt' — 'erant' is imperfect
hints.Perfect: Perfect 3rd plural of 'esse' is 'fuerunt' — 'erant' is imperfect
hints.Pluperfect: Pluperfect 3rd plural of 'esse' is 'fuerant' — 'erant' is imperfect

hints.eo, ire, ii, itum: These are the parts of 'ire' (to go) — not 'esse' (to be)
hints.edo, esse, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere/esse' (to eat) — a different verb with the same infinitive form
hints.erro, errare, erravi, erratum: These are the parts of 'errare' (to wander/err)

hints.Accusative: 'Pastores' could be accusative plural, but here it is the subject — nominative
hints.Vocative: Vocative would be used for direct address — 'Pastores' is the subject here
hints.Genitive: Genitive plural would be 'pastorum' — 'Pastores' is nominative plural

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'pastoris' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'pastoris' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'pastoris' ends in -is

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'regio' — 'regione' with -e ending is ablative
hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'regionem' — 'regione' is ablative after 'in'
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'regionis' — 'regione' is ablative governed by 'in'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'regionis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'regionis' ends in -is
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'regionis' ends in -is
